claim_text: On July 31, the president established 10 percent tariffs on countries with which the US runs a surplus.
subject: Countries with which the US runs a trade surplus
certainty_rationale: The claim uses past-tense language ('established') describing a specific presidential action on a specific date from an official USTR source, indicating a signed executive order or proclamation rather than an announcement of future intent.

claim_text: On July 31, the president established 15 percent tariffs on countries with which the US has a small deficit.
subject: Countries with which the US has a small trade deficit
certainty_rationale: The claim describes a presidential action ('the president established') with a specific effective date and concrete tariff rate, sourced from official USTR communications, indicating an executive action rather than a formal rule publication or law.

claim_text: On July 31, the president established higher tariffs for countries with which the US has large trade deficits.
subject: Countries with which the US has large trade deficits
certainty_rationale: An official announcement by USTR (a US government agency) of a specific presidential action on a specific date establishes certainty at the ANNOUNCED level, below EXECUTIVE_ORDER because the claim does not explicitly reference a signed executive order or proclamation document.

claim_text: The U.S. Department of Commerce preliminarily determined that countervailable subsidies were provided to Hyundai Steel Company and POSCO for certain cold-rolled steel flat products from the Republic of Korea during the period of January 1, 2023, through December 31, 2023.
subject: Cold-rolled steel flat products from Republic of Korea (Hyundai Steel Company and POSCO)
certainty_rationale: A preliminary determination by Commerce is an intermediate stage in the CVD investigation process, published in the Federal Register but not yet a final rule, representing a formal proposal subject to public comment before final determination.
